methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terra Drone operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector, currently trading as a public company on the Tokyo exchange following its IPO. The comparable universe consists of five private peers—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and ACSL—with zero public comparables in the narrow peer set.

The Peer-Median method is not applicable to this valuation. The analysis identified four same-sector peers within Drones & Autonomous, but filtering by stage-adjacency and valuation-tier constraints yielded fewer than three datapoints necessary for robust median analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given Terra Drone's specialized positioning. Without sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds, triangulation via traditional multiples-based peer analysis cannot be executed with analytical confidence.

This analysis cannot produce a supported valuation range without either (1) expansion of the private peer dataset to meet the three-datapoint minimum within tighter stage/tier filters, or (2) application of alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or venture capital method inputs. The key uncertainty is the sparseness of directly comparable public benchmarks and the stage-matching challenge within the private peer set. Further work should focus on obtaining financial metrics from the five identified private peers or identifying additional public-market reference points in adjacent autonomy or aerospace subsectors.
